title: SPOILERS Shinji you suck his character in Q
display_title: SPOILERS Shinji you suck his character in Q
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] "Shinji you suck" - his character in Q             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Nov 20, 2012 5:42 am Here's my translation of interesting post/summary on Shinji's character that is the most exhaustive summary on him specifically I've found. It provided some direct inspiration for how I view Shinji here . Obviously (for those who read that) I don't agree with how the author of this post perceived 2.0 Shinji's end hijinks and some other things so don't go confusing our views. In any case this is very interesting and enlightening summary on guy and why I'd unceremoniously called him "wet bag of dicks" in one comment I wrote around the time I read this analysis/summary for the first time. I'm not providing public link to original post here because I've got impression that Japanese blogosphere doesn't exactly approve of such spreading around links without permission in this manner. If someone wants the link to original text just mention it and I'll send it in pm. Alternatively copypasting the one sentence on which I'm not so sure about as whole on google will probably lead you to the blog just fine as I found it with very general Eva Q impressions googling. TL note: I’ve decided to translate “dame” (which signifies all kinds of negative, unworthy and unwanted things and qualities in general) as worthless each time it appears to keep it clear when the word is used to describe Shinji and others. At times the description sounds a bit odd because of that but it’s not too bad thing. The flip-flop between Shinji-kun and Shinji-san is about how “respectable”/unexpectedly active and grown up he comes up in RoE 2.0 vs the normal "Shinji-kun". *************** Eva Q impression: Shinji bottomed out in Q What is different First thing that draws attention is this: up to this point [3.0] Shinji’s position has been “doesn’t want to pilot Eva but is made to pilot it anyway” – now the situation is “wants to pilot Eva but cannot”. At this point one receives message “things will be different this time”. Shinji’s position felt like the thing that has changed the most on this viewing. Even more so than appearances and personalities of NERV’s staff the depiction of Shinji has been changed. Up to this point we’ve been shown “unjustly oppressed Shinji”. This has been like a premise, of course he’s oppressed, of course he plays the victim. Focus of story has been on finding a place for oneself to be at in a world filled with worthless adults. In response to this mission my interpretations of [Shinji's] achievement level [of this aim] in tv version, old film version and new film version follows. Television series : It looks like in final episode Shinji gained the motivation to live in real world. How well he endured in that reality is not depited. Old film version : Although Shinji has won the motivation to endure in reality it’s worse than expected and drama ends in middle of his sobbing. New film verion : Steady growth through Jo and Ha. From “Shinji-kun” to “Shinji-san”. Clearly satisfied with his real life [guy uses fun term リア充 which means person who is satisfied with one’s offline/IRL life ) In this manner “unjustly oppressed Shinji-kun” has by new film version’s Ha /2.0 reached place where this mission has been accomplished. However this time around “unjustly oppressed Shinji” portrait gets cracked. Even though until now “Shinji as a victim” has been [focus of] portrayal now “Shinji as a perpetrator” is emphasized. This is startling change. Everyone: Worthless humans I think this is because shift in depiction has gone from Eva as story of how “Shinji gets accepted into society” to “how Shinji will live after getting accepted into society”. If you looked at Shinji’s living environment people surrounding him were nearly all worthless. However living a society there’s no guarantee only people around you are worthless. Often there’s worthless part in yourself too. It’s not fair to unilaterally condemn others and raise yourself on a shelf. In Q Shinji defends himself numerous times about stuff he perpetrated with “that wasn’t my intention”. Kaworu’s gentle so for example he goes “you can recover by doing this” and “I’ll assume guilt in your stead”, truthfully he demonstratred devoted wifesque features. But really, was this truly for his sake I wonder. Eventually instead of trying to confront his past Shinji tries to flee. “Run away” was heard in God Warrior appears Tokyo but shouting “running away” as strategic withdrawal is clearly different from “running away” as in turning one’s back to reality. Shinji would be the latter case. Excessively slow witted Shinji This Shinji is genuinely stupid. Even though one can imagine Third Impact took place due to Shinji by watching the beginning 15 minutes [not entirely sure on this but I guess he’s referring to watching first 15mins of 3.0] Shinji himself just doesn’t get it no matter how long [time passes]. One realizes with one look Q’s Ayanami is not Ha’s Ayanami (Poka-Ayanami) but Shinji just keeps on thinking she is Poka-Ayanami. Even though Kaworu says it’s better to stop it falls for Shinji’s deaf ears and he proceeds to pull Lance of Longinus anyway. To be blunt this film’s Shinji is too stupid for words. In comparison to active person in Ha the great difference is bewildering. I felt very frustrated while watching. This is entirely different from empathy I’ve felt up to this point. Generally “Shinji of old movie version” got criticized and called on for continuing to do nothing. In new film version up to Ha this “inactivity" seemed to get challenged and it proceeded well as healthy growth story. Here I want to emphasize that both old and new film version Shinji up to Ha were depicted as persons who draw certain amount of sympathy. For that Shinji to appear empty headed to this lengthy took me completely by surprise. Up till now Shinji was on side of persecuted so whatever he did (including “not doing anything”) I could respect his choice*1 [1 is link to another post on 3.0 which may have exception or something, haven’t read it yet] So what was that I was saying about “Shinji-san”… Q’s Shinji is responsible for his behavior in Ha. “It doesn’t matter what happens to me. It doesn’t matter what happens to the world. But Ayanami, at least Ayanami, I’m going to save!” is something Shinji clearly says in Ha and thus clearly expressed preference for Ayanami over the world. When I first saw Ha I was quite impressed by Shinji making such an absolute decision like saving a girl even at expense of the world. If Shinji was going to fulfill his thought no one would be able to voice a complaint ["about his passivity" is the implication, I gather] . Or, well, though one could make a complaint given that Shinji had already decided he was going to proceed mindlessly no matter what anyone said. However in Q Shinji says “I didn’t intend to destroy world” and tries to pass the buck. For audience which was at that time filled with heat in their hearts and for Misato, his sole supporter within the play, this betrayal is too deep. [Shinji is] Only lacking awareness of one’s worthlessness This time when Shinji took actions he considered good everything backfired. But in contrast between functional conduct in Ha and backfiring Q there surely were some specific differences in Shinji, right... Honestly speaking, personally I can’t see that big difference between the two. I get the feeling most likely the single biggest difference concerning results was between whether the fault lied on the outside or in oneself. Up till now due to worthlessness of people around him it’s been Shinji who has been suffering the most damage. But this time Shinji’s the worthless and his “surroundings” suit the damage. Nevertheless there’s no consciousness of this worthlessness in him so like always he thinks he’s being persecuted and he’s unilaterally the victim. In finale Shinji realizes “all this time I’ve been complaining about circumstances but isn’t it me who is truly worthless?” , becomes stunned and stops all action. It’s a condition where [he] can’t find any value in the world at all. Q Shinji’s exploits To be frank in this work’s climax we don’t see at all Shinji who is aware of his own worthlessness like one we have seen before. Active parties are Kaworu, Asuka, Mari, Ayanami and Misato’s group. In comparison to Shinji even Gendo and Fuyutsuki may be more active. At first viewing I was so taken aback [by everything] I didn’t even notice this but Shinji truly doesn’t do anything meaningful. In a sense this is a feat not even old film version’s Shinji achieved. Even that Shinji in middle of Instrumentality said “but I still want to meet again because I think the feeling I had at that time was true”, rejects Instrumentality, goes back to real world and showcases a good choice. In Q there’s not even that. Incredible! How terrible! (Kensuke) Shinji-san came back Just like in end of old series Shinji has lost motivation to do anything. However this time two persons who weren’t there in theatre version stand by him. In here remains two girls to become his support(?) as a proof of his fight/tenacity until now. Something like “in any case what can be repeated [many times/endlessly] is valuable” is what Kaworu said, suffering up to this point, as process of moving forward while stumbling, bond with Asuka and Ayanami has risen. [I’m iffy with the last sentence: カヲル君も「とにかく何度も反復することが大事」みたいなことを言ってましたが、ここまで悩み、躓きながらも前に進もうとしてきた過程が、アスカや綾波との縁を育んだのです。] Shinji’s stocks have plummeted to the bottom of Earth in this chapter. Most likely he will not fall further than this. From now on the only route avalaible should be up. I look forward to the next, final chapter. ************** I think the guy confuses stupidity with blatant desperate denialism but overall: welp, Shinji's about as sad, pathetic sack here as I expected. I tl'd this couple of days ago when some of the stuff discussed here was not spread at all so I'm a bit late (wanted to finish my another post-EoE fanwank post before posting this) but I think this is still worth a read and I think this thread could be Shinji's character in 3.0 general threat because the "actions" thread is endless piss contest over whether or not it's justified to hold him responsible for 3I in 2.0 In here more general discussion might be possible. Also: mfw sequel theorists will jump on 「とにかく何度も反復することが大事」 See you next time, space cowboy

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 20, 2012 9:10 am Thanks for translating this! I don't agree with some of the points made (Shinji becoming respectable in 2.0? Eeeeeehhhh, this seems more like a logical continuation of exactly where he was already headed if you followed the cynical Luc interpretation like I did. :P) but it's interesting to read this sort of in-depth character assessment from someone who's seen the film. I'm getting the impression this movie is going to be kind of painful to sit through

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:17 pm I understood a lot of Shinji's actions in both NGE and NME, and I also agree it sounds like NME's 3.0 Shinji is desperate and in denial at the end. From what I can gather from the camrip, sounds like he just emotionally snaps at the end by stubbornly doing what (in his opinion) is the "right" thing, logic (and his brain) going out the window. At one point Shinji says "For Kaworu-kun's sake, for everyone's sake", nice parallel with his words/actions in 2.0. Won't really know what to think personally until I watch a subbed camrip. Anyway: Xard wrote: [I’m iffy with the last sentence: カヲル君も「とにかく何度も反復することが大事」みたいなことを言ってましたが、ここまで悩み、躓きながらも前に進もうとしてきた過程が、アスカや綾波との縁を育んだのです。]

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:31 pm So if trying to save Rei ends the world, and trying to save the world ends the world... I guess the only option left is that Shinji tries to save himself in some special, life-changing way and nothing else? Because if that doesn't work, I'm stumped and he might as well go off to a corner somewhere and die because the universe literally has it out for poor, stupid Shinji. Seriously, he should stop caring at that point if nothing works. Like, there should be a scene like in EoE where he sees the mangled Unit 02, but then he has no reaction. "Oh, that's too bad. Whatever." Cue Shinji becoming the insane villain-protagonist. (wuzzup, Macbeth?)

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] "Shinji you suck" - his character i             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:57 pm Xard wrote: Up to this point we’ve been shown “unjustly oppressed Shinji”. This has been like a premise, of course he’s oppressed, of course he plays the victim. Focus of story has been on finding a place for oneself to be at in a world filled with worthless adults.

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:00 pm So I guess we have to ask ourselves, "In Shinji's entire world, is there anyone or anything worth saving? Really?" I'd like to say yes. But what? I mean, there are all sorts of generic answers you can give like, "no one deserves to die," but what would the world Shinji is in be like without him, without the angels and the evas? Is that something we care about seeing anymore? As an audience, do we care enough to see anyone happy? Or are we in it just for the apocalyptic drama, and would find more emotional release by watching it all explode in a ball of red goop again?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:08 pm I'm interested in whether Anno's newfound meanspiritedness towards Shinji actually has a purpose or is simply "ha ha get a life nerds " nonsense which would put Rebuild literally on a level with Michael Bay's Transformers as far as unpleasantness. Relatedly I am interested in the extent to which he is planning to grant Asuka Shikinami or in fact any character who isn't Shinji the dignity he granted Asuka Soryu in EoE. I am also interested in seeing if Yamashita can come up with a dumber design than Mark 09. Character happiness? To be honest at this point I'd settle for a hint that Anno hasn't become a bitter dick thanks to his success.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:01 pm Honestly I'm rooting for Shinji now, no matter how much he fucks up. If he's at least TRYING this time I can forgive just about anything. I mean sure, he's very much a mini-Gendo at this point, but I always liked Gendo so that's not a bad thing. Hell, even if he has slipped fully into EoE mode now I'd be okay with it, because at least this time around the problem actually does stem from his piloting rather the lack thereof.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:15 pm There was a bit in 2.22 (I think) after he comes back from his wandering, where he provokes Misato and she grabs him by the shirt. He gives this really creepy, Gendo-like twisted smile. I'll have to rewatch it. Point being, he's not above manipulating people and provoking reaction gifs long before 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:26 pm Well, I keep wondering how they're going to go with Shinji in 4.0, so we don't hate him. If he just ends up learning that he can be happy no matter what, well that kinda doesn't cut it. It really doesn't seem right that he should be happy after killing all those people, and I don't think the people in the Eva world would be happy to have him around bieng happy. So in addition to gaining a sense of worth, what is he going to do to redeem himself? Shinji needs to earn his sense of worth this time, not just find it. And creepy Gendo like smile? Well in oh so many ways Shinji is his fathers son. He just hasn't learned to be as mean and sneaky about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:26 pm It was 1.11: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:47 pm Grub wrote: So I guess we have to ask ourselves, "In Shinji's entire world, is there anyone or anything worth saving? Really?"

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 20, 2012 3:20 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: It was 1.11:

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:13 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: It was 1.11: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Tue Nov 20, 2012 7:08 pm Grub wrote: That's not a creepy manipulative smile. That's more a "I'm hopelessly depressed and I don't really care about anything in this dumb world, look how emotional you are over it" smile.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:22 pm I know you want to show off your translation, Xard, but do we REALLY need two concurrent Shinji threads?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:27 pm Reichu wrote: I know you want to show off your translation, Xard, but do we REALLY need two concurrent Shinji threads?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:34 pm Xard wrote: I think there's difference between Shinji as character in general and "Shinji's actions" which really seems to be endless debate about whether or not it's justified to hold him responsible for Third Impact

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Tue Nov 20, 2012 9:09 pm Charsi wrote: Let's just agree to disagree on that one, then.

It provided some direct inspiration for how I view Shinji here . Obviously (for those who read that) I don't agree with how the author of this post perceived 2.0 Shinji's end hijinks and some other things so don't go confusing our views. In any case this is very interesting and enlightening summary on guy and why I'd unceremoniously called him "wet bag of dicks" in one comment I wrote around the time I read this analysis/summary for the first time. I'm not providing public link to original post here because I've got impression that Japanese blogosphere doesn't exactly approve of such spreading around links without permission in this manner. If someone wants the link to original text just mention it and I'll send it in pm. Alternatively copypasting the one sentence on which I'm not so sure about as whole on google will probably lead you to the blog just fine as I found it with very general Eva Q impressions googling. Even more so than appearances and personalities of NERV’s staff the depiction of Shinji has been changed. Up to this point we’ve

 intention”. Kaworu’s gentle so for example he goes “you can recover by doing this” and “I’ll assume guilt in your stead”, truthfully he demonstratred devoted wifesque features. But really, was this truly for his sake I wonder. Eventually instead of trying to confront his past Shinji tries to flee. “Run away” was heard in God Warrior appears Tokyo but shouting “running away” as strategic withdrawal is clearly different from “running away” as in turning one’s back to reality. Shinji would be the latter case. Excessively slow witted Shinji This Shinji is genuinely stupid. In comparison to active person in Ha the great difference is bewildering. I felt very frustrated while watching. This is entirely different from empathy I’ve felt up to this point. Generally “Shinji of old movie version” got criticized and called on for continuing to do nothing. It doesn’t matter what happens to the world. But Ayanami, at least Ayanami, I’m going to save!”

… Q’s Shinji is responsible for his behavior in Ha. “It doesn’t matter what happens to me. It doesn’t matter what happens to the world. But Ayanami, at least Ayanami, I’m going to save!” is something Shinji clearly says in Ha and thus clearly expressed preference for Ayanami over the world. When I first saw Ha I was quite impressed by Shinji making such an absolute decision like saving a girl even at expense of the world. If Shinji was going to fulfill his thought no one would be able to voice a complaint ["about his passivity" is the implication, I gather] . Or, well, though one could make a complaint given that Shinji had already decided he was going to proceed mindlessly no matter what anyone said. However in Q Shinji says “I didn’t intend to destroy world” and tries to pass the buck. For audience which was at that time filled with heat in their hearts and for Misato, his sole supporter within the play, this betrayal is too deep. [Shinji is] Only lacking awareness of one’s worthlessness This time when Shinji took actions he considered good everything backfired. But in contrast between functional conduct in Ha and backfiring Q there surely were some specific differences in Shinji, right... Honestly speaking, personally I can’t see that big difference between the two. I get the feeling most likely the single biggest difference concerning results was between whether the fault lied on the outside or in oneself. Up till now due to worthlessness of people around him it’s been Shinji who has been suffering the most damage. But this time Shinji’s the worthless and his “surroundings” suit the damage. Nevertheless there’s no consciousness of this worthlessness in him so like always he thinks he’s being persecuted and he’s unilaterally the victim. In finale Shinji realizes “all this time I’ve been complaining about circumstances but isn’t it me who is truly worthless?” , becomes stunned and stops all action. It’s a condition where [he] can’t find any value in the world at all. Q Shinji’s exploits To be frank in this work’s climax we don’t see at all Shinji who is aware of his own worthlessness like one we have seen before. Active parties are Kaworu, Asuka, Mari, Ayanami and Misato’s group. In comparison to Shinji even Gendo and Fuyutsuki may be more active. At first viewing I was so

At first viewing I was so taken aback [by everything] I didn’t even notice this but Shinji truly doesn’t do anything meaningful. In a sense this is a feat not even old film version’s Shinji achieved. Even that Shinji in middle of Instrumentality said “but I still want to meet again because I think the feeling I had at that time was true”, rejects Instrumentality, goes back to real world and showcases a good choice. In Q there’s not even that. Incredible! How terrible! (Kensuke) Shinji-san came back Just like in end of old series Shinji has lost motivation to do anything. However this time two persons who weren’t there in theatre version stand by him. In here remains two girls to become his support(?) as a proof of his fight/tenacity until now. Something like “in any case what can be repeated [many times/endlessly] is valuable” is what Kaworu said, suffering up to this point, as process of moving forward while stumbling, bond with Asuka and Ayanami has risen. [I’m iffy with the last sentence: カヲル君も「とにかく何度も反復することが大事」みたいなことを言ってましたが、ここまで悩み、躓きながらも前に進もうとしてきた過程が、アスカや綾波との縁を育んだのです。] Shinji’s stocks have plummeted to the bottom of Earth in this chapter. Most likely he will not fall further than this. From now on the only route avalaible should be up. I look forward to the next, final chapter. ************** I think the guy confuses stupidity with blatant desperate denialism but overall: welp, Shinji's about as sad, pathetic sack here as I expected.
